acceptance_targets: [CH10_CRAFT_RECURRENCE_SPRAWL_001] Pass if a cold reader can point to one distinct dramatic function in each surviving major block after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:67: one block establishes the new routine, one block escalates Draco into a behavior he would not have allowed earlier, and Theo's block changes the situation by giving that shift an outside witness. Fail if the revised middle still reads as multiple paraphrases of 'they are gentler now' without a new action, cost, or decision.
[SPECIFIC_PARTICULAR_CH10] Pass if chapter 10 contains zero instances of 'the specific/particular [X] of.' Fail if any instance remains.

[CHAPTER_END_CH10] Pass if the chapter ends within the post-Theo-departure material without a window/darkness/stillness contemplation. Fail if the chapter still ends with a solitary character watching dark descend.
[R-04] Ch10's introduction of Hermione to Theo no longer re-describes both her hair escaping its arrangement and her pushed-up sleeves.
[R-33] Ch10 has em-dashes in no more than 13 prose lines (<=8%).
[R-51] Ch10 has no more than 24 She-opened sentences (<=7%).
[R-66] Ch10 has no more than 24 He-opened sentences (<=7%).
[R-83] Ch10 has no more than 8 'something' instances.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can point to one distinct dramatic function in each surviving major block after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:67: one block establishes the new routine, one block escalates Draco into a behavior he would not have allowed earlier, and Theo's block changes the situation by giving that shift an outside witness. Fail if the revised middle still reads as multiple paraphrases of 'they are gentler now' without a new action, cost, or decision.
findings.problem: The opening kitchen scene already lands the chapter's new register by line 63, but the chapter then spends most of its remaining length certifying the same tenderness in slightly different forms: shared meals, library domesticity, cataloguing Hermione, ordinary lunch, then the closing inventory of kettle/laugh/household warmth. Because those sections mostly restate one state change instead of staging distinct new tests, costs, or irreversible choices, the chapter's center of gravity becomes recurrence and sprawl rather than accumulation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the montage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:67 and the cataloguing sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:117 so each retained block does non-overlapping work. If you keep the library sleep beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:171, make it the chapter's first real escalation in Draco's behavior; if you keep Theo's visit at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:279, let that scene supply the external witness/public consequence instead of using the closing summary at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:345 as another pass of the same proof.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 10 contains zero instances of 'the specific/particular [X] of.' Fail if any instance remains.

evidence.excerpt: 2562: The afternoon passed. The winter light — what there was of it, the sun giving up by half three — came through the library windows at the low amber angle that only happened in early December. In that light the library was the closest thing to a living room the Manor had ever produced. The preservation charms gave the air its permanent base of old paper and dust and the faintly metallic trace of stabilised ink, and layered on top was the tea she'd made an hour ago and the wool of whatever jumper she was wearing and the particular smell of someone else in a space that had been only his for years.

2654: Draco put the kettle on — the repaired one, which held. The silence between them was companionable in a way he'd missed, the specific quality of being with someone who knew him well enough that silence wasn't a failure of conversation but a rest inside it.

2714: Lunch was sandwiches, because that was what the kitchen could produce with the cold-box failing and the preservation charms at half-strength. They ate at the scarred oak table and the conversation moved from the Floo failures to the anchoring theory to Theo's storefront search to the specific fourteenth-century text Granger was translating, and it was ordinary. Three people in a kitchen, eating sandwiches, talking. Granger was sharper with Theo than she was with Draco — not hostile, but more willing to push back, to test his precision with hers. Theo matched her, and Draco sat between them and felt something he hadn't felt in this kitchen in years: the particular warmth of people filling a room that had been empty too long.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2562: The afternoon passed. The winter light — what there was of it, the sun giving up by half three — came through the library windows at the low amber angle that only happened in early December. In that light the library was the closest thing to a living room the Manor had ever produced. The preservation charms gave the air its permanent base of old paper and dust and the faintly metallic trace of stabilised ink, and layered on top was the tea she'd made an hour ago and the wool of whatever jumper she was wearing and the particular smell of someone else in a space that had been only his for years.

findings.problem: Chapter 10 uses 'the particular' at lines 2562 and 2714, and 'the specific quality of being with someone' at line 2654. Three instances in this chapter compound the book-wide pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE all three at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2562, 2654, 2714. At line 2654: the sentence already specifies what the quality is ('being with someone who knew him well enough that silence wasn't a failure of conversation') — cut the framing and let the definition do the work directly.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the chapter ends within the post-Theo-departure material without a window/darkness/stillness contemplation. Fail if the chapter still ends with a solitary character watching dark descend.

evidence.excerpt: 2784: Somewhere above him she was working and her door was open and the library fire she'd started that morning was still burning. He stood at the window for a long time and didn't name anything, and the house settled around him, and the dark came down, and he let it.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
2780: Draco stood in the house he'd grown up in and had not felt at home in since the war and thought about what Theo had said. He thought about the library and the kitchen and the woman who was in both of them. The throw on her shoulders. The laugh hitting the ceiling. The fixed kettle. The way she'd said *come back whenever you want* to his closest friend, casually, as if the Manor were partly hers to open.
2781: 
2782: He went back to the kitchen and washed the mugs. Three of them. He dried them and put them away and wiped the table where the crumbs from their sandwiches were still scattered. The jam she liked was still out. He put that away too, and then stood at the window and looked at the grounds, which were silver with frost under a sky that was almost, not quite, clear enough for stars. The December dark pressed against the glass, and inside the house was warm — not the charged warmth the Manor sometimes produced, just the ordinary warmth of a building that had people in it and fires lit and a kettle that worked.

2784: Somewhere above him she was working and her door was open and the library fire she'd started that morning was still burning. He stood at the window f...

findings.problem: Chapter 10 ends with Draco standing at a window watching darkness descend: 'the house settled around him, and the dark came down, and he let it' (line 2784). This is the sixth chapter to end with a character alone in stillness watching darkness or not sleeping. The window-at-dusk variant is barely distinguishable from the bedroom variant.
findings.rewrite_direction: End chapter 10 at a different point in the scene. The Theo visit that occupies the chapter's middle section provides forward momentum — end closer to Theo's departure, on a line of dialogue or a domestic action, rather than routing to the window-in-darkness template at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2780-2786.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch10's introduction of Hermione to Theo no longer re-describes both her hair escaping its arrangement and her pushed-up sleeves.
findings.evidence: Ch4 line 596: 'sleeves pushed up and three texts spread in a semicircle'; Ch10 line 2682: 'sleeves pushed up, ink on her fingers. Her hair had abandoned whatever arrangement... doing what it always did by midday, which was everything at once'
findings.problem: Ch10 re-describes Hermione's work-mode appearance (sleeves pushed up, unruly hair) using the same traits established in Ch2 (line 234) and Ch4 (line 596). The reader already knows what Hermione looks like at work.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch10 line 2682, reduce the physical inventory. One quick detail is enough to place her in work mode without re-teaching hair and sleeves.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch10 has no more than 8 'something' instances.
findings.problem: 'Something' appears 21 times at 3.0x threshold, substituting vague pronoun for specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace ~13 instances with the specific noun or concept. Keep 'something' only where uncertainty is the point.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
